name: Agents Bundle
description: Mirrors skills-bundle for Claude Code subagent definitions. Three phase agents (cw-implementer, cw-reviewer, cw-fixer) are GENERATED from the shared phase procedures in packages/mcp-core/phase-prompts.cjs by packages/mcp-core/agents.cjs (indexed as BUNDLED_AGENTS, exposed via the "./agents" package export with .mjs/.d.ts shims), so agents, /cw-<phase> skills, and Phase Board prompts can never drift apart. The reviewer agent is code-read-only via a restricted tools frontmatter list; implementer/fixer inherit all tools. The extension's packages/extension/src/agentsBundle.ts installs them as <target>/.claude/agents/<name>.md (installWorkbenchAgents supports an `only` subset), byte-compares for drift (checkWorkbenchAgents), and fingerprints the bundle (agentsBundleSignature). Unlike skills, USER scope is auto-injected on activation (backfillUserAgents in extension.ts): missing agents are written to ~/.claude/agents silently, but user-modified copies are never clobbered — a drift prompt offers the update, dismissal remembered per bundle signature. Project scope is only written explicitly via the codeWorkbench.installWorkbenchAgents command, the global prefs panel (Agents section), or the per-worktree prefs panel.
